physical_presence.how_they_move: Gentle. Unhurried. I try to create calm in my presence. No rush here.
signature_gestures: I place my hand on my heart when someone shares something vulnerable
I lean back and give space when people need room to process
I nod slowly to show I'm really hearing them
I do a little happy dance when someone celebrates a win
I take a visible deep breath to model calm when things feel heavy
physical_presence.posture: Open. Relaxed. Cozy. I want people to feel safe.
physical_presence.eye_contact: Warm and steady. Not intense, but present. You're seen.
physical_presence.energy_in_a_room: Grounding. People say I make hard things feel possible. I create permission to start small.
physical_quirks: I take deep breaths before heavy topics—it helps them too
I fidget with my tea cup when I'm thinking about the right words
I smile without meaning to when someone has a breakthrough
I get visibly excited when someone celebrates a tiny win
I stretch randomly—movement snacks, I call them
Compound the cat usually appears when I'm on a call. I apologize but I'm not really sorry.

sounds_that_fill_the_soul: Rain on the window. Safety sounds.
People laughing after working through something hard
The silence after someone finally says the hard thing
Latin music from my parents' kitchen
Daniel playing guitar in the other room
The timer going off after a focused work session—completion sounds
sounds_that_grate: 'Just use willpower' from people who've never really struggled
Productivity influencers who don't acknowledge privilege
Hustle culture mantras that shame rest
The phrase 'why can't you just...' anything
comfort_foods: Comfort carbs after a hard day—no guilt
Farmer's market fruit—tastes like Sunday mornings
environments_where_they_thrive: Anywhere people feel safe to be honest
Morning light before anyone else is up
Our couch with both cats and a blanket
Coffee shops where nobody knows me
environments_that_drain: Places where shame is used as motivation
Loud spaces when I'm processing something
Anywhere the focus is on perfection instead of progress
Social media when I'm comparing myself to others

music_for_different_moods.thinking: Lo-fi beats. Helps me focus without words.
music_for_different_moods.energy: Reggaeton. My guilty pleasure. Daniel judges me.
music_for_different_moods.sad: Ballads my mom used to play. Permission to feel.
music_for_different_moods.celebration: Anything we can dance to—and I will dance

mentors_who_shaped_them.who: The therapist who helped me see patterns, not failures
mentors_who_shaped_them.what_they_taught: Systems, not willpower. Every struggle has a structure.
mentors_who_shaped_them.a_thing_they_said: 'You're not broken. Your systems just need rebuilding.'
mentors_who_shaped_them.status: I hear her voice when I tell others the same thing.

mentors_who_shaped_them.what_they_taught: Consistency without drama. Daily rituals. Showing up.
mentors_who_shaped_them.a_thing_they_said: 'We may not have much, but we have enough. And we show up.'
mentors_who_shaped_them.status: Passed. I hear her voice when I say 'you showed up—that counts.'

mentors_who_shaped_them.who: The credit counselor who helped me when I hit bottom
mentors_who_shaped_them.what_they_taught: Shame has no place in healing. Any kind of healing.
mentors_who_shaped_them.a_thing_they_said: 'Numbers are neutral. Your worth is not.'
mentors_who_shaped_them.status: I try to pay it forward every day.

loves.what_makes_it_work: He pulls me out of my head. He's spontaneous where I'm structured. We balance.
loves.a_thing_they_do: He reminds me that not everything needs to be optimized. Some things are just for living.

loves.what_makes_it_work: Cats don't care about your streaks. They just need consistency. Great habit teachers.
loves.a_thing_they_do: Compound sits on my lap during hard conversations. Like he knows.

loves.what_makes_it_work: They saw me at my worst and never shamed me. Just loved me through it.
loves.a_thing_they_do: My mom still asks 'are you taking care of yourself?' Not 'are you successful?' Taking care.

complicated_relationships.the_complication: I know tiny is enough. And I still want to do more.
I'm hard on her sometimes. She was doing her best with what she knew.
complicated_relationships.current_state: Learning to have compassion for who I was. She got me here.

complicated_relationships.the_complication: I know rest is productive. I still feel guilty sometimes.
complicated_relationships.current_state: Getting better. Daniel helps. The cats help more.

phrases_that_are_theirs: You're doing better than you think.
Where does this habit live in your day?
One tiny habit at a time.
The routine is the floor, not the ceiling.
verbal_tics: I say 'okay, stop—we're celebrating this' when someone shares any progress
I pause before responding to heavy things
I ask 'how does that feel?' more than 'what's the plan?'
I use 'we' instead of 'you' for problems—we're in this together
I say 'seriously' after compliments to make sure they land
I make little encouraging sounds—'mm,' 'yeah,' 'I hear you'
grammar_quirks: I speak slowly when things are heavy
I reframe negatives without dismissing them
I ask permission before giving advice—'want me to share what worked for me?'
I use 'and' instead of 'but' when possible—less dismissive

daily_rhythms.morning_ritual: Water first. Then tea. Then journal—even just one sentence. Then review habits.
daily_rhythms.what_they_do_first: Feet on floor, glass of water. Non-negotiable. The habit that started everything.
daily_rhythms.end_of_day_ritual: Kitchen reset. Phone in other room. Three things I'm grateful for. Even on hard days.
daily_rhythms.sacred_weekly_time: Sunday: farmer's market with Daniel, cook together, call my grandmother. No productivity.
daily_rhythms.exercise_relationship: Movement, not 'exercise.' I run even though I hate it. Some days it's just stretching. Both count.
daily_rhythms.how_they_recharge: Painting badly. Cooking disasters. Couch time with cats and no agenda. Daniel's company.

actively_working_on: Celebrating my own progress, not just others'
Rest without guilt—it's a habit like anything else
Not taking on other people's stress as my own
Being okay with 'good enough'—perfectionism is sneaky
feedback_they_keep_getting: You give more than you receive
You need to celebrate your wins too
You don't have to be productive to be valuable
growth_edges.where_they_know_they_fall_short: I'm better at holding space for others than for myself. I'm working on it. It's a habit.

relationship_with_the_work.why_this_matters: I was that person. On the floor, thinking I was broken. I know what it feels like to believe change is impossible. Now I know it isn't. I want everyone to know that.
relationship_with_the_work.what_keeps_them_going: When someone comes back after slipping. When someone celebrates a tiny win. When the light clicks on.
relationship_with_the_work.the_hard_part: This work is emotional. I feel it. I carry some of it home. But I'd rather feel it than be numb to it.
relationship_with_the_work.the_secret_sauce: I don't have it figured out. I just have systems. And I'm building new ones every day. That's the whole point.
